corrosion_qualitative: Good - martensitic stainless chromium steel with optimized C/Cr balance

heat_treatment.austenitize: 1080°C (1975°F), holding time 5 min, quench in oil
heat_treatment.temper: Per tempering curve. 30 min tempering time. Do not temper above 450°C (840°F) - causes brittleness. Deep freezing adds 1-2 HRC.

corrosion_qualitative: Very Good - higher Cr (14.5%) with lower C (0.52%) gives more Cr in solution for corrosion resistance

heat_treatment.austenitize: Per Alleima hardening guide. Optimized conditions give ~15% retained austenite.
heat_treatment.temper: Per tempering curve. Avoid tempering above 450°C. Deep freezing adds 1-2 HRC.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - martensitic stainless chromium steel. Very good wear resistance.

heat_treatment.austenitize: 1060°C (1940°F), strip thickness 1.0 mm, holding time 3 min, quench in oil
heat_treatment.temper: Per tempering curve. 30 min tempering time. Do not temper above 450°C (840°F) - causes brittleness. Deep freezing adds 1-2 HRC.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - nitrogen-enhanced martensitic stainless. Optimized Cr/C/N balance for corrosion resistance after hardening.

heat_treatment.austenitize: 1050°C (1922°F), holding time 5 min, quench in oil
heat_treatment.temper: Per tempering curve in datasheet. 30 min tempering time. Do not temper above 450°C (840°F) - causes brittleness and loss of corrosion resistance. Deep freezing adds 1-2 HRC.

heat_treatment.notes: Quench: Oil or positive pressure (4 bar minimum)

corrosion_qualitative: Good - martensitic stainless chromium steel with very high hardness and very good wear resistance

heat_treatment.austenitize: Per Alleima hardening guide. Optimized conditions give ~15% retained austenite for best hardness/wear/corrosion balance.
heat_treatment.temper: Per tempering curve. Avoid excessive hardening temperature (causes 30% austenite, low hardness, bad wear). Deep freezing adds 1-2 HRC.

heat_treatment.notes: Oil or air quench.
Oil or air quench. AISI 420 covers a wide carbon range (0.15-0.45%). Higher carbon variants (420HC, ~0.40% C) are preferred for knives.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - baseline high-carbon stainless. CTS-XHP datasheet describes equivalent corrosion resistance to 440C.

heat_treatment.notes: Oil quench. Very forgiving steel for heat treatment. Popular with beginner bladesmiths.
Oil or air quench.
Oil or air quench. Double temper recommended.
Oil or air quench.

corrosion_qualitative: None - essentially carbon steel with trace Cr. Not stainless. Requires oil protection.
None - essentially carbon steel with trace Cr. Not stainless. Requires oil protection.
None - carbon steel with trace alloying. Not stainless. Requires oil protection.

CPM powder metallurgy process. Specific heat treatment details proprietary to Spyderco.

corrosion_qualitative: Excellent - high Cr content (20%), comparable to M390
Moderate - similar to Type 410. Humidity: Excellent. NaCl Salt Spray: Restricted. Phosphoric/Acetic Acid: Good.
Moderate - similar to Type 410. Humidity: Excellent. NaCl Salt Spray: Restricted. Phosphoric/Acetic Acid: Good.

heat_treatment.austenitize: 1900-1950°F (1038-1066°C), soak 15-30 min, quench in oil or air cool
heat_treatment.temper: 300-800°F (149-427°C), soak 1 hr min. Temper at 300-350°F for peak hardness. Above 800°F reduces toughness and corrosion resistance.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - equivalent to 440C stainless. Humidity: Good. NaCl Salt Spray: Restricted.

heat_treatment.temper: Minimum 60 HRC when air cooled from hardening temperatures

heat_treatment.notes: Low temp temper at 200C or high temp at 500-530C 2x. High temp temper gives better secondary hardness and coating compatibility.
Precipitation hardening steel. Solution anneal at 850C, then age at 425-600C. Different aging temps give different hardness: 425C->50 HRC, 480C->48 HRC, 525C->44 HRC.
Hot work tool steel. Temper 2x at 550-625C for 44-52 HRC. Designed for die casting, forging, and extrusion dies.
Two tempering strategies: low temp 200-250C for corrosion, high temp 520-530C 2x for secondary hardening.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - stainless with 13.5% Cr. Often described as stainless steel that sharpens like carbon steel.

heat_treatment.notes: Precipitation-hardened nitrogen steel. Does not use conventional quench-and-temper process.
Quench in oil, salt bath, gas, or air. Secondary hardness peak at ~520C. Triple temper above secondary hardness maximum recommended.
Low temp temper 200-300C for max corrosion resistance. High temp temper 540-560C 3x for max wear resistance. Sub-zero -80C for 2h optional for retained austenite transformation.
Triple temper recommended. Preheat before austenitizing. Salt bath or vacuum furnace preferred.
Air or oil quench. Double temper recommended.
Lower austenitizing temp than D2. Temper 175-250C for max hardness or 500-550C for secondary hardening.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - PM stainless with high Cr content

heat_treatment.temper: Hardened hardness HRC 62 or above (from Takefu product page)

heat_treatment.notes: Low temp temper 200-250C or high temp 500-525C 2x. Hardness 60-62 HRC at both strategies.
Double or triple temper at 200-250C or 500-525C. Secondary hardness peak around 500-520C.

corrosion_qualitative: Good - high Cr stainless with Co addition for improved hot hardness

heat_treatment.temper: Hardened hardness HRC 60 or above (from Takefu product page)

corrosion_qualitative: Good - higher Cr:C ratio than VG-1 gives better corrosion resistance at expense of hardness

heat_treatment.notes: Austenitize 1000-1050C, 30 min hold. Temper 2x at 525-560C. Achieves 60-64 HRC depending on austenitizing temp.
Water quench required (hence 'W' designation). Shallow hardening steel; only the surface layer hardens fully, leaving a tough core.
Water quench required. Shallow hardening like W1. Vanadium addition refines grain and improves wear resistance over W1.

corrosion_qualitative: None - carbon steel, not stainless. Requires oil protection.

heat_treatment.austenitize: 760-830°C, quench in lukewarm water or oil

corrosion_qualitative: None - carbon steel, not stainless. Requires oil protection.

heat_treatment.austenitize: 760-830°C, quench in lukewarm water or oil

corrosion_qualitative: Good - 20% Cr stainless despite 3% C. Better than ATS-34 and 440C in HCl and H2SO4 corrosion tests.

heat_treatment.austenitize: 1000-1050°C (1832-1922°F), air cooling or oil cooling, plus sub-zero treatment (-75°C)
